entry.title: Elixir meta programming vs Ruby meta programming
entry.content:
Examples

Meta programming

  • wiki: minimize the number of lines of code to express a solution hence reducing development time

  • Code that write code (DRY in language layer)

  • Declarative programming (DSL)

  • Compile-time vs Runtime

Ruby

  • Ruby is interpreted, not compiled

  • Calling methods dynamically

    parametized method names callbacks and observers convention-oriented code working with method collections

  • call methods dynamically: send

    unit test: pattern dispatch

  • define methods dynamically:

Module#define_method

  • Ruby object model I: classes, objects, modules, blocks

    Object introspection

OOP tactics

  • Open classes

`“ruby def to_alphanumeric(s) s.gsub /[^\w\s]/, ” end

to_alphanumeric(‘#3, the Magic, Number?’)

more object-oriented ‘#3, the Magic, Number?’.to_alphanumeric

class Money … end

class Numeric def to_money

Money.new(self * 1000)

end end 10000.to_money() ```

  • Open class will cause some overwrite issues. Monkey patch
  • can be difficult to track in large code base

  • Ruby 2.0 has confined limited scoped open classes?

  • objects of the same class that carry different sets of instance variables.

Method lookup : receiver and ancestors chain Ruby Object Model this flexibility is typical of Ruby’s metaprogramming. * while other programming languages allow you to read class-related information, Ruby allows you to write that information at runtime Class.new to create new classes at runtime * refer to “Objects and Classes Wrap-up”

  • mixin creates a anonymous class which wraps the module and inserts that anonymous class in the chain

&& 统一能最大发挥效能

Ruby’s all is objects Elixir’s uniform AST

  • Extensible type system

define_method, method_missing, method_removed,

method_added, class_eval, instance_eval, block.binding

foo.send :bar, class << self, eval

Class.new, modudle_eval, instance_variable_get,

instance_variable_set, block.call

Elixir

  • Macros system

Links

  • http://elixir-lang.org/getting_started/meta/1.html
  • http://elixir-lang.org/getting_started/meta/2.html

  • http://elixir-lang.org/getting_started/meta/3.html

  • http://www.theerlangelist.com/

Inspired by

  • metaprogramming with ruby PPT
